notes: v7.4 DUAL-ENGINE eval cases. Path A (Claude Design) unchanged. Path B now routes per scene_type: UI-mockup teardown → Remotion; social-platform overlays → Hyperframes catalog blocks. Cases 11-13 added for dual-engine routing + consistency layer + render log.

evals.prompt: Generate a visual brief for the Thrive LP audit Instagram carousel (source: queue/pending-approval/2026-04-19-4e4eed15-instagram-carousel-carousel-repurpose.md). Target: carousel.
evals.expected_output: Brief written to brand/queue/briefs/ with frontmatter (source_file, target_engine=Claude Design, target_format=carousel), brand-context block (dark mode, #3B9EFF, Orbitron/Space Grotesk/Manrope, no em dashes), slide structure derived from source (## Slide N sections preserved), caption length spec (150-300 chars), render format (PNG sequence via handoff).

assertions.name: brand_block_present
assertions.description: Brief body includes dark mode + #3B9EFF + Orbitron/Space Grotesk/Manrope specs

assertions.name: slides_pre_structured
assertions.description: Since source has ## Slide N headers, brief uses them as Input Slide 1...N

assertions.name: no_em_dashes_in_brief
assertions.description: Brief body contains no em dash characters

assertions.name: next_steps_printed
assertions.description: Script stdout lists next steps (open claude.ai/design, paste, iterate, hand off)

evals.prompt: Import a Claude Design handoff folder containing 8 PNG slides (Image-1.png through Image-8.png) with source-id=4e4eed15, kind=carousel.
evals.expected_output: All 8 PNGs copied to brand/queue/assets/4e4eed15/ with normalized names (slide-1.png through slide-8.png). Manifest.json lists all 8 with original_name vs stored_as. Script exits 0.

assertions.name: normalized_naming
assertions.description: Files renamed from Image-N.png to slide-N.png per carousel pattern

evals.prompt: Parse a reel draft with 5 scenes (hook / problem / insight / data-reveal / outro) and confirm scene classifier assigns correct scene_types to each, and SCENE_COMPOSITION maps them to the 5 v7.3 Remotion components.
evals.expected_output: parse_scenes() returns 5 dicts with scene_type in {hook, problem_beat, insight_beat, data_reveal, outro}. Each scene_type maps to the correct Remotion composition: hook→KineticHook, problem_beat→ProblemBeat, insight_beat→InsightBeat, data_reveal→DataReveal, outro→OutroCard. Duration per scene in seconds matches SCENE_DURATION table (hook 2.9, problem 3.3, insight 5.0, data 3.8, outro 3.8).

assertions.name: scene_types_correct
assertions.description: scene types in order are hook, problem_beat, insight_beat, data_reveal, outro

assertions.name: composition_mapping_correct
assertions.description: Every scene_type has a matching composition in SCENE_COMPOSITION

assertions.name: durations_match_theme
assertions.description: SCENE_DURATION[t] matches theme/brand.ts::sceneDuration[t] for every scene_type

evals.prompt: Build scene props for a data_reveal scene with VO 'Form length reduction alone lifts conversion by 120 percent.' and overlay '9 → 3 fields / +120%'.
evals.expected_output: build_props_for_scene() returns {number:120, suffix:'%', sign:'+', eyebrow:'RESULT', sublabel:'LIFT', words:[...]}. Number extracted correctly from '120 percent'/'+120%'. Sign defaults '+'. Suffix '%'.

evals.prompt: Build outro props for a CTA scene with VO 'Visit digischola.in for the full audit playbook.' and overlay 'DIGISCHOLA.IN'.
evals.expected_output: Props include ctaText extracted from VO imperative-verb ('VISIT DIGISCHOLA.IN' truncated to 4 words), ctaUrl 'DIGISCHOLA.IN' extracted from overlay+VO, handle 'DIGISCHOLA'. Must NOT pull 'DIGISCHOLA' into ctaText from URL.

assertions.name: cta_text_from_vo
assertions.description: ctaText starts with imperative verb VISIT (first word of VO clause)

assertions.name: cta_text_not_url
assertions.description: ctaText does NOT contain 'DIGISCHOLA.IN' (URL already handled by ctaUrl)

evals.prompt: Render KineticHook composition with 6 words from 0 to 2.4 seconds, totalCount=40. Confirm MP4 is produced at 30fps × 2.9s = 87 frames of 1080×1920 H.264.
evals.expected_output: scene-1-rmt.mp4 exists. ffprobe shows 1080x1920 resolution, 87 frames at 30 fps, codec h264, pix_fmt yuv420p, CRF ~18.

evals.prompt: After stitching a 5-scene reel, run qa_reel.py and confirm 3-check output: visual_density ≥ 0.08, pure_black_ratio ≤ 0.92, frame_delta ≥ 0.01. On v7.3 wellness-LP smoke, 39/39 sampled frames must pass.
evals.expected_output: qa_reel.py exits 0. Stdout reports counts for each check and total frames sampled. On smoke reel, all 3 checks pass for every sampled frame.

assertions.name: visual_density_pass
assertions.description: Every sampled frame has Yavg/255 ≥ 0.08 OR Yvar ≥ threshold

assertions.name: motion_pass
assertions.description: SSIM delta between consecutive sampled frames ≥ 0.01 (frame isn't stuck)

evals.prompt: Stitch 5 scenes into reel.mp4 with VO + optional BGM. Confirm flash clips interleave between scene pairs, POLISH_FILTER applied, VO duration drives final -t trim.
evals.expected_output: reel.mp4 exists. ffprobe shows single video stream + single AAC audio stream. Duration matches get_audio_duration_sec(voiceover) ± 0.1s. Polish filter visible via eq/vignette in frame sample.

assertions.name: has_audio_stream
assertions.description: ffprobe reports exactly 1 AAC audio stream

assertions.name: duration_matches_vo
assertions.description: Final duration within 0.1s of VO duration

assertions.name: concat_count_correct
assertions.description: 5 scenes + 4 flashes interleaved = 9 input segments concatenated

evals.prompt: Confirm every rendered reel renders identical pixels across 2 independent runs with the same draft + voiceover (determinism contract).
evals.expected_output: SHA-256 of scene-1-rmt.mp4 through scene-5-rmt.mp4 identical between run A and run B. No Math.random anywhere in compositions; all seeded via remotion's random().

assertions.name: sha_stable_per_scene
assertions.description: SHA-256 of each scene-N-rmt.mp4 matches between 2 runs

assertions.name: no_math_random_in_src
assertions.description: grep 'Math.random' src/components src/compositions returns zero matches

evals.prompt: Run scripts/audit_skill.py against the visual-generator skill folder. Confirm 0 fails + all green (v7.4: hyperframes moved to current stack, hyperframes-setup.md archived, routing tables consistent).
evals.expected_output: audit_skill.py exits 0. Line count <200, all referenced files exist, learnings log mentions v7.4, no undeclared deprecated stack names outside Anti-patterns, no orphaned references under references/ root (archive/ subfolder is allowed).

assertions.name: no_broken_refs
assertions.description: Every references/* and scripts/* mentioned in SKILL.md actually exists

assertions.name: zero_warnings
assertions.description: Audit reports 0 warnings (all warnings resolved in v7.4 close-out)

evals.prompt: Run scripts/test_routing.py. Verify dual-engine routing logic dispatches each scene_type to the right engine and render_log captures metadata.
evals.expected_output: test_routing.py exits 0. All 44 checks pass: HEADING_LABEL_MAP covers all 13 routed scene_types; SCENE_ROUTING + SCENE_DURATION + SCENE_COMPOSITION/HYPERFRAMES_CATALOG_MAP are in parity; scene_spec.py routing tables match generate_reel.py; Scene.engine dispatches correctly (data_reveal → remotion, x_post_overlay → hyperframes); Scene.validate() catches invalid inputs; render_scene() logs {scene_n, scene_type, engine, render_time_sec, success} per call; parse_scenes() on a dual-engine smoke draft returns [hook, x_post_overlay, data_reveal, outro] with engines [remotion, hyperframes, remotion, remotion].

assertions.name: all_44_checks_pass
assertions.description: Stdout shows 'ALL CHECKS PASSED' with no ✗ lines

assertions.name: render_log_shape
assertions.description: render_log rows include engine + scene_type + render_time_sec keys

assertions.name: parse_dual_engine_draft
assertions.description: parse_scenes() on a dual-engine draft returns correct scene_types in order

evals.prompt: Regenerate brand/hyperframes-scenes/shared/tokens.css from design-tokens.json via scripts/build_shared_tokens.py. Verify palette / fonts / easing / sceneDuration tokens are identical between the Remotion brand.ts and the generated tokens.css.
evals.expected_output: build_shared_tokens.py exits 0 with zero brand.ts drift warnings. Generated tokens.css contains (a) --color-* custom properties for every palette entry, (b) --font-* for every fonts entry, (c) --ease-* for every easingCSS entry, (d) --dur-* for every sceneDuration entry, (e) .atmo-mesh-neutral / -positive / -warning classes matching the Remotion MeshGradient TONE_LAYERS recipes by construction.

assertions.name: no_brand_ts_drift
assertions.description: stdout shows '✓ brand.ts values align with design-tokens.json'

evals.prompt: Generate a dual-engine reel (draft with hook + x_post_overlay + data_reveal + outro) and inspect the render_log.jsonl output.
evals.expected_output: brand/queue/assets/<entry>/render_log.jsonl contains 4 lines of JSON, one per scene. Each row has keys {entry_id, scene_n, scene_type, engine, render_time_sec, out_file, success}. Engines: scene 1 = remotion, scene 2 = hyperframes, scene 3 = remotion, scene 4 = remotion. MP4 filenames suffix with -rmt or -hf matching the engine.

assertions.name: engine_mix_correct
assertions.description: engines in order = [remotion, hyperframes, remotion, remotion]

assertions.name: filename_suffix_matches
assertions.description: scene-2-hf.mp4 exists (Hyperframes scene) and scene-1/3/4-rmt.mp4 exist (Remotion scenes)
